content.problemStatement:
Given an integer NNN, let FNF_NFN​ be the set of factors of NNN. e.g., F6={1,2,3,6}F_6 = \{1,2,3,6\}F6​={1,2,3,6}.

The radical of an integer NNN, denoted by rad(N)\text{rad}(N)rad(N), is defined as the product of the distinct prime factors of NNN. e.g., rad(12)=2×3=6\text{rad}(12) = 2 \times 3 = 6rad(12)=2×3=6.

Define an **antichain** of a set SSS of integers to be a subset of SSS such that for any two elements xxx and yyy in the antichain, rad(x)\text{rad}(x)rad(x) and rad(y)\text{rad}(y)rad(y) do not divide each other. e.g., antichains in F6F_6F6​ are {}\{\}{}, {1}\{1\}{1}, {2}\{2\}{2}, {3}\{3\}{3}, {6}\{6\}{6} and {2,3}\{2,3\}{2,3}.

Given NNN, find the size of the largest antichain of FNF_NFN​, and the number of antichains of FNF_NFN​ of that size. Since the answers can be large, print both of them modulo (109+7)(10^9+7)(109+7).

e.g., if N=6N = 6N=6, the largest antichain is of size 2, and there is only 1 antichain of that size.

Since NNN can be large, the input is the prime factorization of NNN. The input has two arrays of size MMM: **base** and **power**, and N=base1power1×base2power2×⋯×baseMpowerMN = \text{base}_1^{\text{power}_1} \times \text{base}_2^{\text{power}_2} \times \dots \times \text{base}_M^{\text{power}_M}N=base1power1​​×base2power2​​×⋯×baseMpowerM​​.


Input Format

The first line contains TTT, the number of test cases. Description of the TTT test cases follows.
Each test case starts with a single integer MMM.
The next MMM lines each contain 2 integers separated by a space. The ithi^{th}ith line contains basei\text{base}_ibasei​ and poweri\text{power}_ipoweri​.


Output Format

For each test case, output one line containing two space-separated integers, respectively the size of the largest antichain modulo (109+7)(10^9+7)(109+7) and the number of antichains of that size, again modulo (109+7)(10^9+7)(109+7).
constraints: basei is a prime number for all 1 <= i <= M
For any 1 <= i != j <= M, basei != basej
content.inputFormat: The first line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with a single integer M. The next M lines each contain 2 integers separated by a space. The ith line contains basei and poweri.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output one line containing two space-separated integers, respectively the size of the largest antichain modulo (10^9+7) and the number of antichains of that size, again modulo (10^9+7).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1. The largest antichain size is 2, and there is only one of it {2,3}.
Example case 2. The largest antichain size is 2, and there are two of those: {2,3} and {4,3}.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we first need to understand the concept of antichains and how to count them. An antichain is a subset of a set such that no two elements in the subset are comparable. In this case, we are dealing with the set of factors of a number N, and two factors are comparable if one is a multiple of the other. We can use a combinatorial approach to count the number of antichains of a given size.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * M * 2^M) — the time complexity is dominated by the number of possible subsets of the set of prime factors.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(M) — the space complexity is dominated by the storage of the prime factors and their powers.

code.Python3:
# COMPLETE, RUNNABLE Python3 code
# Uses input() or sys.stdin
# Handles all T test cases
import sys
MOD = 1000000007
input = sys.stdin.readline
for _ in range(int(input())):
    M = int(input())
    base = []
    power = []
    for _ in range(M):
        b, p = map(int, input().split())
        base.append(b)
        power.append(p)
    base.sort()
    dp = [0] * (1 << M)
    dp[0] = 1
    for mask in range(1, 1 << M):
        i = mask & -mask
        i = i.bit_length() - 1
        dp[mask] = dp[mask ^ (1 << i)]
        for j in range(i + 1, M):
            if (mask & (1 << j)) and base[j] % base[i] == 0:
                dp[mask] = (dp[mask] + dp[mask ^ (1 << j)]) % MOD
    max_size = 0
    max_count = 0
    for mask in range(1 << M):
        size = bin(mask).count('1')
        if size > max_size:
            max_size = size
            max_count = dp[mask]
        elif size == max_size:
            max_count = (max_count + dp[mask]) % MOD
    print(max_size, max_count)
